column1: Different backgrounds for different projects
column9: A lot of other developers at my work experience the same issue with the latest sourcetree version 3.3.6. We had to downgrade to 3.1.2 which is the latest stable version.a†I think 3.3.7 behaves in the same way. as 3.3.6, so is also unstable.a†The jira menu below asks me to report a bug on a specific sourcetree version, however 3.3.6/7 isn't an option on there, so I will submit the latest version that does come up in the dropdown - 3.2.2, however I am sure that version is actually fine. Not sure why the latest versions don't come up in the dropdown. Within the actual sourcetree it prompts me to upgrade to 3.3.7, so I assume this dropdown on Jira is out of date.a†ISSUE DESCRIPTION - {color:#FF0000}*Affects 3.3.6 and 3.3.7*{color}:When you update to another branch, the view still shows that you are on the previous branch. Once I close the app completely and reopen, only then it is then obvious that I have checked out another branch. So it does update in the background, however the view isn't updating correctly.a†Similarly when you stage files, it shows them in the unstaged section until I shut the app down, reopen it and then find them in the staged section.a†a†Similarly creating a new branch - it doesn't show the new branch on the side bar at all until shutting down and reopening the app.a†Im using windows 10 if this helps.

column1: Custom action parameter $BRANCH not always properly replaced
column9: Creating pull requests through Sourcetree is super buggy.When I right-click on a branch and select Pull Request, it tells me that I "...don't have any remotes which have extended integration settings configured". So, I go into repository settings and specify an endpoint. Great.Then, when I go back and right-click on the branch and select Pull Request, it shows an error dialog that says, "Unable to open your web browser to '', do you have a default web browser set?"a†I've forgotten how I've worked around this in the past, but it usually involves purchasing a new office window and monitor. Later, when I reopen Sourcetree after closing or restart, everything gets wiped and I have to repeat the above steps all over again.This is beyond frustrating. When this button is clicked another error message is shown "Unable to open your web browser to <remote URL>, do you have a default web browser set?"Going to Setting as suggested by the first error above, selecting the your remote, clicking "Edit", then without changing anything, clicking OK to close that window, and then OK again to close the "repository settings" window, fixes the error and pull requests can be created again, but only temporarily, until Sourcetree is Done.The next session of Sourcetree will still show the error when creating a pull request.a†_Note_: This error happens in version *3.3.4*, not in version 3.2.4, but when creating the issue here and typing 3.3.4 in the "Affects Version/s" edit box, I get an error saying that that version doesn't exist.a†There's a community question related to this:https://community.atlassian.com/t5/Sourcetree-questions/Unable-to-Create-Pull-Request-Default-Browser-Empty/qaq-p/1213475

column1: Differing behavior - launching terminal
column9: The option "Refresh automatically when files change" no longer works in 3.3.7. It was working fine in 3.3.6. What used to happen was when SourceTree gained focus, if anything had changed it would auto refresh. This no longer occurs. (NOTE: option "Refresh when application is not in focus" is disabled)In fact even operations initiated in Sourcetree no longer refresh. For example creating or applying a stash doesn't actually update the repo view, you have to manually hit F5 to force a refresh. Again this was working fine in 3.3.6.This is causing major issues for us as it means you are operating in a stale view of the repo unless you keep constantly hitting F5 to force a full refresh.

column9: There was a vulnerability in Sourcetree for macOS and windows that could reveal Git user credentials via maliciously crafted URL by an attacker, This vulnerability is triggered when the affected version of Git is used to execute a git clone command on a malicious URL.Affected versions of Atlassian Sourcetree For Mac allow remote attackers to view sensitive information via an Information Disclosure vulnerability in Git.Affected versions of Atlassian Sourcetree for Windows allow remote attackers to view sensitive information via an Information Disclosure vulnerability in Git.*Affected versions:* * Windows version 3.2.2 and earlier*Fixed versions:* * Windows versiona†3.3.9*Released Sourcetree for Windows versiona†3.3.9*a†that contains fixes for these issues and can be downloaded froma†[https://www.sourcetreeapp.com/].For additional details, see thea†[full advisory|https://confluence.atlassian.com/pages/viewpage.action?spaceKey=SOURCETREEKB&title=SourceTree+Security+Advisory+2020-09-02]

column1: Logical tree level for local branches
column9: There was an argument injection vulnerability in SourceTree for Windows introduced through git-lfs. An attacker could create a malicious repository which, after being cloned in SourceTree for Windows and enabled with git-lfs, is able to exploit this issue to gain code execution on the system.a†*This is the result of an incomplete fix for CVE-2020-27955**Affected versions:* * Versiona†3.4.2 and earliera†*Fix* * You can download the latest version ofa†the [standard installer|https://product-downloads.atlassian.com/software/sourcetree/windows/ga/SourceTreeSetup-3.3.9.exe] or the [enterprise installer|https://product-downloads.atlassian.com/software/sourcetree/windows/ga/SourcetreeEnterpriseSetup_3.3.9.msi].a†For additional details, see the [full advisory|https://confluence.atlassian.com/display/SOURCETREEKB/SourceTree+for+Windows+Security+Advisory+24th+March+2021]

When I view the files diffs (staging or commit), if I scroll down to the bottom of the file, it will freeze the UI and I have to close the process to restart.Not all files trigger this freeze, but when one does, it will freeze everytime when I scrolldown (so I can reproduce the bug easily on my end). column371: 19/May/2022 8:23 PM;ab65dd59d2d5;This still affects version 3.2.6. It was very difficult to figure out what is going on. Fortunately, I was able to find a post [https://stackoverflow.com/questions/25887799/using-source-tree-rebase-with-conflict-rebase-doesnt-work-after-conflict-reso] on what to do during a rebase.It should be more obvious from the UI itself what to do next.;;;
column372: 13/Jun/2022 12:41 PM;ec478821a07a;Still an issue in 3.4.8.a† The way to continue a rebase should not be:a† google why you are stuck in rebase without any obvious button to click, find this issue, read the linked stackoverflow link, click the "Actions"-> "Continue Rebase".Best would be to have two buttons to choose from after resolving conflicts in a rebase: "Continue Rebase" and "Abort Rebase" instead of the "Commit" button as you probably want to commit all your changes after resolving all conflicts in all the steps in the rebase process. The "Commit" button can stay if you think it is a use case, but please add "Continue Rebase" and "Abort Rebase" buttons as going to the menu items are really unintuitive way of continuing a "next->next->next->finish" type of a process.a†Additionally, the "Push changes immediately to -" checkbox throws an error if you "rebase" -> "resolve conflicts" -> check the "Push.." checkbox -> hit "Commit". Please disable this checkbox when rebase is in progress since it apparently does not make sense.a†And a another minor issue, if you happen to check the "Push changes immediately to -" and hit "Commit" after resolving a conflict from a rebase, then you get an info popup with an "Cancel" button, but it should be an "Ok" button since you are not cancelling any action, the popup showing:---------------------------"Push Failed""Your commit succeeded but your push failed. To retry your push, use the Push toolbar button".---------------------------;;;

column1: SourceTree Shortcut Start In path invalid
column9: Hello.I recently added a new system drive to my ASUS ROG G751-JY, an M.2 NVMe SSD, in the hopes of gaining a lot of performance with Git and SourceTree, among other tools, by moving my projects from the stock HDD over to an SSD. The stock SSD was out of space, so I got this new one to replace it.When I moved my repository to the new SSD, every SourceTree operation ran extremely slowly, as in it stalled for 20+ seconds just to commit a tiny change or update a view. I tried putting the stock SSD back in and doing the same things, thinking it was the switch from a non-NVMe SSD to an NVMe SSD, but it was the same bad performance.The SSD(s) sit on an M.2 PCI-e interface; the stock one is non-NVMe and the new one is NVMe, so we're also testing the difference between those two. The mainboard's chips limit the bandwidth of the interface to PCI-e 2.0 4X speeds, which should still be magnitudes better than my HDD, but you can see the performance I get.*More info below the results, but you need to see these to care :D**+Time trials of SourceTree:+*¬†*HDD 5400 RPM* (ROG G751-JY {*}stock{*}) - *SATA II slot*¬†- (Seq. read/write benchmarks: {*}101/93{*}):- Show initial "File Status"¬† - *37.2* seconds- Stage 1 file, few changes - *3.5* seconds- Show History¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬†- *5.3* seconds- Random update on File Status - *1.2* seconds*SSD* *NVMe* (ROG G751-JY {*}stock{*}) - *M.2 slot* (Seq. read/write benchmarks: {*}1140/850{*}):- Show initial "File Status"¬† - *44.5* seconds{color:#FF0000} (+20%){color}, sometimes 125 seconds {color:#FF0000}(+336%){color}- Stage 1 file, few changes - *22.3* seconds {color:#FF0000}(+637%){color}, sometimes 43 seconds {color:#FF0000}(+1229%){color}- Show History¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† - *5.3* seconds {color:#ff8b00}(same){color}, sometimes 40 seconds {color:#FF0000}(+811%){color}- Random update on File Status - *18* seconds {color:#FF0000}(+1500%){color}*SSD* *NVMe* ({*}SN850{*}) - *M.2 slot* (Seq. read/write benchmarks: {*}1615/1459{*}):- Show initial "File Status"¬† - *41.5* seconds {color:#FF0000}(+12%){color}- Stage 1 file, few changes - *21.5* seconds {color:#FF0000}(+614%){color}- Show History¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬†- *5.2* seconds {color:#00875a}(-1%){color}- Random update on File Status - *15* seconds {color:#FF0000}(+1250%){color}*SSD*¬†{*}NVMe{*}{*}{*} ({*}SN850{*}) - *External USB 3.0 NVMe interface* (Seq. read/write benchmarks: {*}475/454{*}):- Show initial "File Status" - *45.1* seconds {color:#FF0000}(+21%){color}- Stage 1 file, few changes ¬†- *23.5* seconds {color:#FF0000}(671+%){color}- Show History ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† - *45.3* seconds {color:#FF0000}(+855%){color}- Random update on File Status - *20* seconds {color:#FF0000}(+1666%){color}+*Result Elaboration and Discussion*+I only included the sequential read/write marks; for the non-sequential tests, the SSDs are 40-400+ times faster than the HDD. I tried on the system partition and a secondary partition.During these stalls, the CPU was at 10-40% usage (spread evenly across the cores) and the SSD at 0-2%. SourceTree would disappear from the Task Manager for most of the time of these stalls; it does that on HDD as well, but usually only for a short while before returning and updating the UI, but in these cases, SourceTree was gone from Task Manager for these long stalls until it finished. I'm on ethernet cable, so it's not Wi-Fi...nothing is stalled on any performance monitors.And I did multiple tests for each of these results, made sure to turn off virus scanners, also adding exclusions for the folders for safety, everything else turned off and shut down. Ensured they were indexed by Windows, as well, like the original repo, and checked folder security settings. I always run SourceTree as Administrator. All that jazz.I'm down to it being Git or SourceTree, that simply do not function well with my harddrives or their ports, or the drivers/interfaces for my ports/drives/mainboard not being optimized for what Git/SourceTree is doing. I've checked everything with these SSDs. They're formatted and adjusted properly, zipping and unzipping is fast and CPU-capped, games install and run using up to 425 MB/sec, and that's just what I've measured "in the field", but benchmarks are as expected for the ports, as well. It's just Git/SourceTree.I know that everything on this machine is 6+ years old, but when everything else seems to be able to take advantage of the new disks, I'm running out of reasons to blame my system.
column371: 06/May/2022 3:08 PM;f92a01ff2e91;I've chosen the wrong version. I'm on the newest one SourceTree itself thinks is the newest, which is 3.3.4.3796.And the Stock SSD is just PCI-e, NOT NVMe. Sorry for the error. That means I also tested with a non-NVMe PCI-e SSD on the M.2 interface.;;;

column1: Allow Multiselect Cherry-pick using Interactive Rebase
column9: Hi Atlassian,I'm sure you're already aware but would like to bring to your attention the use of Bitbucket App Passwords in Source Tree as a security risk. I'd call it a high security risk because the app password is needed every time we do a git clone and is immediately visible in plain text upon pasting it behind the username. Further to that, after each git clone, in the settings of the project in source tree, you can actually see the full app password in plain text as well. The plain text app password is visible even on workstations with only standard user access. Meaning that even though our environment is setup where all the devs are standard users (instead of local admin users), an attacker who has successfully got into one of our dev workstations (especially if they are familiar with Source Tree), can actually grab the app password in plain text, save it somewhere alongside the path, setup the same path on their Source Tree on their workstation with that app password and start doing commits, push, pulls etc as that user. If that user uses the same app password for each of their project, then essentially and attacker could use that same app password to do git clones from several projects. If that user uses different app passwords for each of their project, then they might not be aware if an attacker has compromised one of their app passwords to do git clones for whichever project. All this pretty much defeats the purpose of turning on multi-factor authentication.a†If there is a better way or more best practice way to authenticate from source tree to Bitbucket (or if our company is just using app passwords wrong entirely), I'm more than happy to hear how we should be doing it instead and push out a new implementation to our devs. But as is, I couldn't figure out a sustainable way to authenticate with Source Tree apart from using app passwords. Have tried using ssh for authentication but that broke after just one day of testing.a†Microsoft has plans to deprecate basic authentication and legacy authentication (this year I believe) which to me means that big corps will start moving away from basic authentication, legacy authentication, app passwords entirely eventually. I would like to know if there are plans to build 2FA authentication into the Source Tree app when using with Bitbucket in the near future as that would be absolutely critical security wise I would think.a†Regards,Brandon Chuah

column1: Clone Remote - Failure to validate/accept remote repository path
column9: I was recently trying to determine why Sourcetree was so slow and in the process I ran into an issue with the Shortcut Start In path being invalid.The last part of the path was pointing to an older version folder that no longer existed. That older version may have been 3.6.6.I manually updated the path to the latest version folder and all is working fine. I am not sure if this will help with performance but it certainly will eliminate this as a contributing factor.Please look into why the Start In path is not updated as it should when installing an Sourcetree update.Thank you.

column1: source tree closing when adding new clone
column9: We have several projects that use a common submodule, albeit often on different branches.(1) Current versions of SourceTree will not open multiple instances of a submodule if the name is the same (Sourcetree 1.9 and before had no problem with this).(2) More egregiously, if you have one instance open, and request to open a second (separate) instance of the submodule (associated with a different project/repo, and possibly on a different branch), SourceTree *simply takes you to the first open instance!* This opens up all sorts of problems with the user thinking they are working on one branch of the submodule, when in fact they are modifying another branch.
column371: 25/Apr/2022 5:22 PM;5673d7adb7c0;To clarify point (1): Current versions of SourceTree will not open multiple instances of a given submodule (those instances being associated with different git repos) since the name is the same (Sourcetree 1.9 and before had no problem with this).;;;

column9: Now that SourceTree has an interactive rebase dialog, the next natural step is to allow us to multi-select several commits from a second branch and "cherry-pick" them onto the current branch.This can also be viewed as a rebase operation where a number of the commits from the second branch are already marked to be "skipped." But in general, rebase and cherry-pick should be viewed from a technical perspective as interactive rebase operations. For example, and because it makes for popular word-games at Git Tech parties: * "rebase" cherry-picks all the commits from one branch onto another. * "cherry-pick" rebases a single commit from one branch onto another.

column387: 14/Sep/2022 2:29 PM;957e7f42e58c;Hi Mukesh,we debugged the calls from Sourcetree to Bitbucket using a proxy and found the problem.*Test Setup* - Bitbucket DC (you are using bitbucket.org for your tests)8.3.1 - Sourcetree 3.4.9 - Sourcetree configured with a proxy to log the http calls to Bitbucket - Sourcetree configured to use proxy and to connect to BitBucke DC with user HssF*Steps* - open Sourcetree => personal repos are listed{code:java}GET /rest/api/1.0/repos?projectname=~HssF&permission=REPO_READ&visibility=&limit=30&start=0{code} - enable checkbox for organization repos{code:java}GET /rest/api/1.0/projects?limit=1000&start=0{code}=> json repsonse contains array of available projects with key/name as attributes{code:java}..."values": [{        "key": "MJ",        "name": "Atlassian"...{code}=> project list is populated with project *key* instead of *name* - doing a search projects are searched by name (parameter projectname) but *key* (MJ) is used as value for parameter{code:java}GET /rest/api/1.0/repos?projectname=MJ&permission=REPO_READ&visibility=&limit=30&start=0{code}=> result is an empty list => Sourcetree shows no repositories=> modifying call using parameter *projectkey* instead of *projectname* returns the correct list{code:java}GET /rest/api/1.0/repos?projectkey=MJ&permission=REPO_READ&visibility=&limit=30&start=0{code}*Suggestions* - filling the list of projets with the projectname and using the projectname parameter would enhance readablity for the user - alternatively use projectkey instead projectname parameter would fix the problem too, but most users aren't aware of the project keys - adding a hint below the search box that only private repositories are searched would help users to understand that search isn't global, even better would be to search accross all repos in all projects the user has access toohope that helps,Frank;;;
column388: 19/Sep/2022 9:19 AM;957e7f42e58c;Hi Mukesh,any news after our analysis? Anything missing? We have next meeting with the team tomorrow and would be great if we are able to tell them some progress.thanksFrank;;;
column389: 26/Sep/2022 10:46 AM;957e7f42e58c;Hi Mukesh,any feedback? We have invested in tracing down the bug and feedback would be really great. We have the next meeting tomorrow and I can't give my users any new information :(.regards,Franka†;;;

column391: 20/Oct/2022 4:10 PM;698877135425;Hi [~827229d3466d]a†We have fixed this issue and it will be available in upcoming release of Sourcetree.Thank you [~957e7f42e58c] for your valuable inputs.Dropdown allow user to select the project by name.User can search the repositories under a project by providing repository name in the search box.!image-2022-10-20-21-41-05-339.png|width=547,height=282!;;;

column371: 29/Mar/2022 4:25 PM;1f69ea7f5282;This still does not work correctly in the latest version (3.4.8)a† As stated, the last version where this worked properly with gerrit is version 3.1.3.a† When our config for origin is set to refs/heads/*:refs/for/*, what we expect to see whenever we get the push dialog (or when checking the push automatically in the commit dialog) is for whatever current branch we are one (master, someBranch etc) for the push location to be as configured in the configuration file (so in this case, our local master would attempt to push @ refs/for/master, same for someBranch which should attempt to push to refs/for/someBranch.a† This version is somewhat better in that we can at least type in the remote branch where we want to push, but this is not as functionnal as 3.1.3 which would automatically populate the remote branch with the proper refs/for/YourBranchName value.;;;

column1: Bitbucket Oauth and Basic Authentication Not Working After Following Resolution Steps
column9: We have the issue that some users are able to authenticate on Bitbucket Server using git or the weblogin, but not Sourcetree.¬†The error message is:Authentication failedFailed to check login for user [username] You do not have permission to access URL 'https://.../rest/api/1.0/users/username' please check the user credentials associated with this account. The request returned with status code 'Unauthorized'. Authentication failed. Please check your credentials and try again.¬†We changed the Bitbucket server back to http and checked the http-traffic.There we see for the authentication a Base64-String containing username and password.This is the case for git as well as for Sourcetree.¬†But when decoding the Sourcetree Base64-String you can see that certain characters are replaced by "?". So far this is the case for "¬ß" as well as for german umlauts (√§,√∂,√ľ).¬†As git is doing the correct encoding this seems to be a Bug in Sourcetree.This happens with 3.4.1 as well as 3.4.6.¬†For us the workaround is to avoid these characters in the password.

column376: 24/May/2022 12:44 PM;e85ff1f4514c;Yes, As you said _"If no files are checked in the "No staging" view or no files are staged, you can`t commit and without a commit the files won't get pushed either."_a†So checkbox is also disabled as the commit button. These two will be in sync. If you can not commit then you can not push too.;;;
column377: 24/May/2022 6:17 PM;cl1450260031;@Vipin YadavBut can you explain me what this is good for in thoughts of UI/UX please?I only see disadvantages: # You can't change the checkbox when in the "No staging" view. Neither activate ...!image-2022-05-24-19-45-15-972.png!... nor deactivate ita†!image-2022-05-24-19-50-20-415.png! # It's not possible to deactivate it when no files are staged. So if you wanted to activate it only for one commit, in the worse case it's not possible to deactivate it afterwards because there are no files to stage anymore. And even when there are files to stage you'll have to stage them, then uncheck the option and finally unstage your files again. So you might forget to uncheck it before the next commit and acidently push all your commits.!image-2022-05-24-19-49-06-891.png!a†Why do you force the user to do this? I would like to understand what the developers intended by adding this restriction.(It seems like I'm not allowed to add attachments / images, so please see https://stackoverflow.com/questions/71721460/#72321511 for them);;;
column378: 06/Jun/2022 8:58 AM;ed9b22895829;I was hoping the latest release would fix this but I'm pleased that a workaround is possible by changing the view and staging files.*When using the _No staging_ view it should be possible to select and deselect the _Push changes immediately to [branch]_ checkbox so that I can decide if my commit should be immediately pushed to the origin branch.*Currently, if a file is staged in split view, when you change back to _No staging_ then the file is ticked and the _Push changes immediately to [branch]_ checkbox is enabled and remains enabled even if you untick all staged files. The Commit button reacts as expected - it's only possible to commit if at least one file has been committed.It looks like the _Push changes immediately to [branch]_ checkbox is only enabled when a file is staged in split view.It should also be enabled if a file is ticked in _No staging_ too. Optionally, it could also be disabled if no files are ticked.;;;
column379: 30/Sep/2022 12:53 AM;684342547c12;I agree with @Christian Ludwig and @79IT_cms - in previous versions before 3.4.8 of SourceTree, you could check the "push changes immediately to [branch]" when you were in the No Staging view by having one or more files selected.a† That makes sense to me... if you're not using staging, I'm confused why it's a problem to allow the checkbox to be selected.a† The commit button is disabled until you select one or more files, seems like the "push changes immediately to [branch]" checkbox should follow the same behavior... but it stays disabled regardless of files being selected or not.;;;

column1: case sensitivity
column9: When there is a space in the filename or file path and one tries to run a custom action on that file (Custom Action script with the parameter of $FILE) the script doesn't work.a† In version 3.4.5 and earlier, Sourcetree would surround the parameter $FILE with quotation marks (when there is a space in the filename).a† Starting with version 3.4.6 it doesn't surround the filename with quotation marks so the filename that is input for the script is wrong (the space in the filename splits the input name into two).a† This is very important for our office since we use these scripts extensively for our work.a† (trusted_tester).

column1: Stash view should show dates
column9: It appears that SourceTree 3.4.7 comes bundled with Pageant 0.70, but the released Putty version of Pageant is 0.76 at this time. The difference in the two causes the keys to be lost and an error message to be displayed when pushing to the server. Replacing the SourceTree/tools/putty files with the latest from the release Putty fixes this issue.

column9: I think you have this error already logged in ~2016 and apparently resolved but now it's back for me and have tried various troubleshooting steps like ensuring that the ssh-agent command runs for various users in different shells and terminals, but can't reproduce the error anywhere else.Specifically, this happens if my agent is OpenSSH (not pageant), and when Git Version is set to System (not embedded). Switching away either of those doesn't throw anymore.!image-2022-02-13-11-42-44-526.png!
column371: 20/Feb/2022 6:45 AM;52113ca36425;This might be the same problem that I have. When SourceTree starts it produces error: 'ssh-agent' failed with code 1: System.NullReferenceException.This might be because SourceTree it is trying to start ssh-agent. I don't want or need it to start ssh-agent because I am using the OpenSSH that comes with Windows 10 and the ssh-agent runs as a service and starts automatically. Unfortunately I can't disable the setting: Automatically start SSH agent when SourceTree opens.Even though I get the error on startup SourceTree works fine otherwise. I can connect to repositories OK using ssh, and can git pull and git push without being asked for SSH passphrase.;;;
column372: 01/Jun/2022 10:06 AM;203638631d80;I have similar issue, like @Brett McKenzie :a†when SourceTree starts (or Tools > Options menu is Done) it produces error: 'ssh-agent' failed with code 1: System.NullReferenceException, with Windows 10/11 OpenSSH.It *still* happens in both versions 3.4.8 and 3.4.9!https://community.atlassian.com/t5/image/serverpage/image-id/186689i484766A7E9D1A71F/image-size/large?v=v2&px=999!;;;

column1: SourceTree does not start (as.atlassian.com)
column9: Sourcetree has troubles when two branches has the same name with character case difference (Bitbucket handles it fine).It keeps telling me the local branch is ahead of the origin when they're at the same commit.Deleting the branch with the wrong name is a solution but it shouldn't mess with how Sourcetree handles branches

column1: can not access repositories, authentication always fails
column9: I have been using Sourcetree with OAuth via bitbucket without any problems.I had to change my password for bitbucket login and since then I am getting the following error while trying to perform any action via sourcetree.*"git -c diff.mnemonicprefix=false -c core.quotepath=false --no-optional-locks fetch --no-tags origin**remote: Too many invalid password attempts. Log in at https://id.atlassian.com/ to restore access.**fatal: unable to access 'https://bitbucket.org/xxx/xxx.git/': The requested URL returned error: 403**Completed with errors, see above."*In order to resolve this, I have tried the following ways but had no success. * Created an app password and tried to use it in source tree (via Basic auth) but no help (although app password worked in git client/command line) * I have tried logging out of bitbucket.com and logging in multiple times, but get the same error each time. * Deleted all instances of bitbucket & sourcetree from a windows password manager. * Deleted {{passwd}}a†file froma†{{C:\Users\[user]\AppData\Local\Atlassian\SourceTree}} * In Tools > Options > Git tab then update my Git version using the "Update Embedded" also tried switching to a system in the Tools > Options > Git Version in the same * Reinstalled sourcetree several times still the error persists.Neither of the suggested workarounds has worked. Any help is appreciated.

column1: Please make warning LESS SCARY about a detached head
column9: Sometimes it happens thea†{{ssh-agent.exe}} process takes to much CPU (about 33% on my Intel i5 2.5GHz) and I have to restart the SourceTree. Note, I have authentication by the ssh key, so maybe disabling the ssh-agent.exe would help, see SRCTREEWIN-6154.a†Similar problems is reported for the GitHub, too, see [http://stackoverflow.com/questions/26547024/github-for-windows-ssh-agent-exe-using-high-cpu-100-disk] And check the {{moggizx}} comment about the SourceTree - upvoted 6 times.
column371: 04/Feb/2022 1:58 PM;e767f37200a0;Still a bug in v3.4.7. If this is using 25% of CPU at say 45W for a laptop, the amount of power used by this bug globally must be huge.;;;
column372: 28/Mar/2022 2:55 PM;320471a3f588;Can also confirm (in v3.4.8, using Git v2.35.0).I've noticed it since changing the `SSH Client` setting in Sourcetree from 'PuTTY / Plink' to OpenSSH, if that's relevant.a† But it's happening on two different machines and seems to start some time after the computer resumes from sleep.a† Closing Sourcetree and re-opening it resolves the issue for that session.a†;;;
column373: 26/Apr/2022 8:14 AM;c74825ee094e;Can also confirm it for v3.4.8.Almost always after resuming from sleep it's at least using 8 % of CPU. The most annoying part is, that my notebook fan constantly runs on some high rpm due to that.So besides the power waste it probably reduces the lifetime of my notebook either.;;;
column374: 14/Nov/2022 6:40 AM;4361b82558da;I confirm it's also present on v3.4.9And it may happen anytime (not only after resuming from sleep);;;
column375: 01/Dec/2022 9:15 AM;b5d1656d4245;Also still present in v3.4.10.To avoid noise from my notebook's fan and to save power, I developed a little monitoring service that kills the ssh-agent.exe as soon as it exceeds a defined limit for a defined time. Sourcecode and installer available on GitHub: [https://github.com/MA-Maddin/SSHAgentMonitor]a†;;;
column376: 02/Feb/2023 7:56 AM;b6f7ae1acc71;This is still an issue.;;;

column1: Spaces in File path of Custom Action
column9: Local Repositoriesa†Bookmark Bar does not refresh. It shows Modifications, but once the repository in question is commited / pushed, the Sidebar is not updated. It keeps showing the repositories as modified until the application is restarted.a†Also, if the sidebar was opened with "no changes" and changes performed while the application is running are not reflected properly.a†
column371: 02/Feb/2022 4:31 PM;8bf9f3842729;Applies for GIT, not sure if relevant.Also, repositories are nested in folders.;;;
column372: 28/Feb/2022 11:50 AM;8bf9f3842729;Any news on this? To better outline the Issue, I've uploaded a series of screenshots:a†1.) Local Repository is unchanged, everything is "fine" after application start:a†https://static.equinox-solutions.de/share/sourcetree/13774/Capture-1.PNG2.) Local Repository is changed - the bookmark-bar stays in "unchanged" state.Expectation: The Repository bookmark should refresh, telling me "Hey, there is something you should commit".https://static.equinox-solutions.de/share/sourcetree/13774/Capture-2.PNG3.) Application is restarted - now the bookmarkbar is telling me "Hey, there is something you should commit"!https://static.equinox-solutions.de/share/sourcetree/13774/Capture-3.PNG4.) Changes are commited and pushed - bookmarkbar is not catching up, telling me "Hey, there is something you should commit"https://static.equinox-solutions.de/share/sourcetree/13774/Capture-4.PNGThis makes it somewhat unhandy to identify changes. I need to go through any repositiory manually to see, if there is something I should commit.a†If it cannot be directly wired to "live updates", it would at least be handy if the user could configure a refresh interval for the bookmark-bar, i.e. every 5 minutes or sth.a†;;;

column1: Add extended integration with Team Foundation Server (TFS) 2015 and Visual Studio Online
column9: When I go on the Clone section and I put the URL, the program just crash. The problem happen with SourceTree V3.4.7. But when I was using an older version (SourceTree V3.4.4) I never have this issu.The program is on a Windows PC.¬†The list of all what I tried for the moment:-Run with Administrator mode.-Uninstall and install again the program.-Uninstall and install again the program with administrator mode.-Delete all account register on the program.¬†
column371: 27/Jan/2022 4:47 AM;e85ff1f4514c;Thanks for reporting. Could you please attach the log file too. It can be found here: %localappdata%\Atlassian\Sourcetree\sourcetree.log;;;
column372: 27/Jan/2022 1:50 PM;dfdf8c6411a2;Hi,I finally I fixed the problem so the log file will probably not good for helping you.¬†This is what I did, but I don't know how it have fix the problem.1-Uninstall SourceTree V3.4.72-Delete the folder Atlassian who is located at C:\Users\USERNAME\AppData\Local3-Download SourceTree Version 3.4.4. You can find the set up file at https://product-downloads.atlassian.com/software/sourcetree/windows/ga/SourceTreeSetup-3.4.4.exe4-Delete SourceTree Version 3.4.4.5-Delete Atlassian file again ( I don't know if it was necessary)6-Download again SourceTree Version 3.4.7.¬†Probably you can replace step 4 to 6, by only go into the settings of SourceTree V3.4.4 and making an update.¬†Is it possible to have an explanation?;;;

column1: Make Linux version available
column9: h2. DetailWhen set proxy to `OS default`, the `http.proxy` config of Git was set to `http.proxy=http://www.google.com:80`.h2. Steps to reproduce# Set proxy in Tool - Options# `git config --global -l` and here's the right proxy config `http.proxy=http://127.0.0.1:1080`# Clear proxy in Tool - Options (set to `OS default`)# `git config --global -l` and here's `http.proxy=http://www.google.com:80`.Reproduced in v3.4.6.

column1: Errors During Installation
column9: I wanted to follow a perfectly normal and feasible scenario: check out an old commit, to test for comparison.a†Trying that resulted in a warning that scared the SHlT out of me:"Checking out this commit will create a detached HEAD, and you will no longer be on any branch (unless you subsequently create a new one)."a†I only dared to move on after searching the web about this action, and making an extra backup.a†This is unnecessary and even WRONG.The warning should tell what COULD happen, and how to avoid that.a†It got even WORSE when I RETURNED to the previous head, without any changes."You are about to leave a detached HEAD and your current commit isn't reachable by others branches, which means they will not appear in the log after this action."a†That sounds asif a disaster has ALREADY happened.a†Please give more sensible and CORRECT warnings, without unnecessary SCARING people and WASTING their time.a†a†

column1: Cannot Remove Keys from SSH Agent
column9: I have been using Sourcetree 3.4.4.a† We use custom actions extensively for many different uses.a† When I would run custom action with the $FILE parameter that has a space in the file path or file name, the Sourecetree would automatically add quotes around the entire file path.a† For example when I try to run lockfile.bat on 'testfile unit2.txt' it would run as 'lockfile.bat "testfile unit2.txt"'.a† That way the entire filename is sent to the batch as one unit.However when we updated to Sourcetree version 3.4.6 this has stopped.a† Now it would run 'lockfile.bat testfile unit2.txt' and it would send two separate inputs 1)testfile and 2) unit2.txtI am not sure what changed in between these versions but is that something that will be recovered in new versions?

column9: I am using SourceTree on Windows.a† I have SSH keys to validate the server connection.a† So, when I start Sourcetree it launches the SSH Agent which then asks me for the passwords for each key. That is fine.However, while I can see an option in SourceTree to "Add SSH Key" I cannot see how to remove one.a† My actual problem is that, due to a misleading error message, I re-added the key and it now has the same key multiple times and asks for too many passwords at start-up!a† But, more generally, I would like to be able to list and remove keys from the SSH agent.I have tried using command linea†{{ssh-add}}a†but it says{quote}Error connecting to agent: No such file or directory{quote}(Tried this both with windows CMD shell and in the Git bash terminal that SourceTree launches)I see a runninga†{{ssh-agent.exe}}a†process in Task Manager when SourceTree is running - in the Details tab. But in the Services tab it says the service is stopped. So, I guess SourceTree has started it but how do I connect to it? Alternatively, how to tell SourceTree not to start it's own but use the Windows Service?I am using the OpenSSH SSH client (in SourceTree options).If SourceTree is managing the SSH Agent (as it seems to be) and given it gives me the option to add a key, it should either include options to remove one or to launch some other UI (or even CLI) that can do so.

column9: Sourcetree for Windows does not update the version string in the registry after auto-update. This is unfortunate when using package managers, such as winget, which rely on the registry to retrieve the current version.a†On the screenshot you can see the version as reported by "Apps & Features" and on the right the version reported by Sourcetree.!image-2021-11-28-17-47-17-086.png!a†

column374: 16/Dec/2021 11:32 AM;e6ee12999410;Sorry for the delay!This is a screenshot showing different versions in "Apps & Features" and Sourcetree itself: [https://pasteboard.co/moek1GRHn8Cq.png]Steps to reproduce: # Install a previous version of Sourcetree, e.g. [3.4.5|[https://product-downloads.atlassian.com/software/sourcetree/windows/ga/SourceTreeSetup-3.4.5.exe]]. # Open Sourcetree and update via Tools > Options > Updates > Check for Updates. Follow the process to update Sourcetree. # Open Sourcetree again, go to Help > About Sourcetree. It should show version 3.4.7 (at the time of writing). # Open Windows Settings > Apps & Features. Click on the Sourcetree entry and look at the version number. It is still at 3.4.5.;;;

column1: Stash + "Keep staged changes" does not keep changes
column9: If the "Show whitespace" option is turned on, diff view displays dot symbols for both tabs (four dots) and spaces. If the identation in the file is changed from tab to spaces (and 4 spaces are used for each tab), the user cannot see the whitespace change.I'd suggest to replace tab with "aĽ" plus (tab size - 1) spaces (3 for tab size 4), so the alignment stays the same.Example (tab size = 4):{{aĽa†a†line with one tab}}{{aĽa†a†}}{{aĽa†a†line with two tabs}}{{aĽa†a†}}{{aĽa†a†aĽa†a†}}{{line with three tabs}}a†a†

column1: We use virustotal find sourcetree 3.4.5 has Trojan Horse on it
column9: Generally, teams have one branch that's considered the main line of development (usually this is the "master" branch, or if using git-flow the "develop" branch). It would be helpful if that branch was visually distinctive in the log view's graph.Today I had to inspect the history of several branches, and every time I checked out a branch our "develop" line changed color and moved around. This made it difficult to keep track of what happened after doing several operations.I imagine two ways to do this, possibly done together:1. Keep the "main" branch fixed as the left-most branch. If the branch at the top of the list isn't the main branch, then its graph starts in the "second" column. (mockup attached)2. Choose a color for the main branch. If the main branch is set to blue, then no other branch will be colored blue.Both of these imply that the user can choose, on a per-repository basis, which branch is considered the main one.

column9: SourceTree 3.4.6 is shipping outdated putty binaries:app-3.4.6\tools\putty\ pageant.exe, plink.exe, puttygen.exe"Pageant: Release 0.70.2-Sourcetree""plink: Release 0.70.2-Sourcetree""PuTTYgen Release 0.70.2-Sourcetree"Not sure if 0.70.2-Sourcetree is 0.70 or if fixes get backported.a†Current Putty Version 0.76 (released 2021-07-17)Sourcetree Putty Version 0.70 (released 2017-07-08)https://www.chiark.greenend.org.uk/~sgtatham/putty/changes.html
column371: 30/Aug/2021 1:25 PM;e4b613531814;+1. Not only have there been many security fixes in PuTTY tools since 0.70, but versions 0.75 onwards also default to a new version of the .ppk key format, which older versions of the suite (such as those shipping with SourceTree) can't read at all.;;;

column375: 29/Mar/2022 2:51 AM;e13ea5c874ec;We are running 3.4.8 and Forticlient is still reporting as Vulnerable for both Log4 and OpenSSL.Looks like when it updates it leaves the old log4net dll.!image-2022-03-29-13-51-18-810.png!;;;
column376: 02/Aug/2022 8:17 AM;70df92533dd0;I have 3.4.9 and FortiClient is still reporting it as a critical vulnerability. It claims C:\Program Files (x86)\Atlassian\Sourcetree\log4net.dll is version 2.0.8.0(1). Perhaps the only way to fix this is to fully uninstall SourceTree and re-install?a†;;;
column377: 02/Aug/2022 8:27 AM;70df92533dd0;Actually I found, according to Windows "Add/Remove Programs", I had 2 versions of SourceTree - 3.4.1 and 3.4.7. I uninstalled 3.4.1 and that whole C:\Program Files (x86)\Atlassian directory disappeared.I suspect that now SourceTree is now installed under AppData\Local. Curiously I am running 3.4.9 so I don't know why the installer hasn't updated its version - maybe it's some kind of legacy issue that won't get tidied up until the next major release.Anyway, deleting the old 3.4.1 version from "Add/Remove Programs" worked for me :) so I hope this helps others.;;;

column374: 06/Nov/2021 7:34 PM;0112b9c55dea;I started to see this issue on v3.4.6.0 when I updated to Windows 11 (21H2). I thought it had something to do with the video driver, but I have the same result if I run the app with the integrated card (Intel UHD Graphics) or with the dedicated card (NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3080).¬†I'm not sure if Windows 11 has something to do, but I think the issue is around the¬†hardware acceleration setting of the app. I [disabled the hardware acceleration option|https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/desktop/wpf/graphics-multimedia/graphics-rendering-registry-settings?view=netframeworkdesktop-4.8#disable-hardware-acceleration-option] system-wide, and the problem completely disaperad. column1: AskPass.exe error
column9: I am working with ANSI-encoded files with contents containing German umlauts. After setting Sourcetree's default encoding to "Windows-1252", the diffs are shown correctly in the internal diff viewer. However, staging of hunks or lines that involve umlauts fails with the git error "patch does not apply".Looking into %LOCALAPPDATA%\Atlassian\SourceTree\LastFailedPatch.txt I found that Sourcetree converts the diff from ANSI to UTF8 to generate the patch file (i.e. umlauts are shown correctly when viewing the patch file as UTF8-encoded). Git does not care about encoding, so it will try to apply the UTF8-encoded patch to the ANSI-encoded original file. This fails whenever the patch contains characters with representations that differ between ANSI and UTF8.In my opinion the correct way to fix this bug would be to stop converting the patch content from whatever encoding it has to UTF8, because git will *not* convert it back to its original encoding before trying to apply it. As a quick test, I converteda†%LOCALAPPDATA%\Atlassian\SourceTree\LastFailedPatch.txt back to ANSI encoding and executed git apply manually, which then worked.a†A similar issue for an older version of Sourcetree has been reported asa†SRCTREEWIN-8768.

column1: Local branch does not appear in push dialog box
column9: SourceTree requires that one and only one SSH key can be configured. For those of us who use multiple external system (e.g. github.com, gitlab.com, self hosted sever, etc), a typical security practice is to use different SSH keys for each system, so that if anyone key was compromised, only one system is potentially affected. OpenSSH supports this quite easily by setting up the config to use different SSH keys based on host, e.g.a†{{Host gitlab.com}}{{a† HostName gitlab.com}}{{a† IdentityFile ~/.ssh/id_rsa_gitlab}}{{Host github.com}}{{a† HostName github.com}}{{a† IdentityFile ~/.ssh/id_rsa_github}}a†This works with git on the command line, but not in SourceTree.a†

column1: Sourcetree constantly asks for GitHub credentials
column9: When using an SSH key with a passphrase, if SourceTree is open for a long time (e.g. all day), the SSH key is initially held open, but at some point appears to timeout.a† After that, if you try to push, any hooks run, but the push just hangs until it eventually times out, but doesn't prompt for the SSH passphrase (or clearly state that's the problem).To workaround, if the push appears to be taking too long, cancel the push, then open and close options, which re-prompts for the SSH passphrase, then re-push.

column1: Can't activate focused button with the space key
column9: I am using Sourcetree with Git, for Framemaker files, which are binary files.a† Framemaker is a desktop publishing software for technical writers. We have a "book" file which groups multiple individual Framemaker files together (each of which represents a chapter in the book). Framemaker has a function where you can change some attribute of all of chapters in a book, such as changing the conditions in all the chapters, or updating the page numbering in all of the chapters, or clearing the change bars in all of the chapters. When performing some of these "group" actions, each chapter is opened quickly, changed, and then saved again. During this process I get an error message that one of the chapters could not be saved. When I look at the File Status in SourceTree, it shows as a Removed/Deleted file in the Unstaged Files section. I then have to Discard the deletiong and re-do the group action. When I redo the group action, a different file will then get deleted, and so on, over and over again.This is not specific to my laptop - there are 5 writers in our group, and it happens to all of us.This does appear to be specific to either SourceTree or Git, because this only started happening once we started using SourceTree and Git (our files were previously in a Subversion, and we never had this issue).If I move the book and all its chapters OUTSIDE of the Git directory/Sourcetree, and do the same group action, it works fine.a†I updated to the latest version of Sourcetree (3.4.5), because I noticed in the release notes that there was a fix for an issue that SORT of sounded like it might have something to do with this ("Fixed staging modified files with paths longer than 260 characters stages the file as 'Removed'), but updating did not resolve the problem. Also, I checked my file paths, and none are longer than 100 characters.a†

column1: Unable to remove submodules that located at folder which contains space
column9: If a new repository tab is added with the "Add repository" option using a path with duplicated slashes like this one:C:\\MyFolder\\MyRepoThe repository will be added (as expected), but then the open in "Explorer" button will fail to open the right folder and it will just open the default FileExplorer path.It is common to find paths with duplicated slashes because thats how it is stored in json files, and windows is usually able to normalize the slashes to make things work.Normalizing is just removing duplicated slashes with just a few exceptions like UNC paths at the begining of the path:\\servername\mySharedFolder\myRepoSourceTree should be able to open the "Explorer" in any case, other functionality might be having the same normalization problems if they operate with the repo path.
column371: 23/Jun/2021 7:40 AM;55a972f1726a;Correction, the path example to reproduce would be with double slashes like this:C:\\MyFolder\\MyOtherFolder\\MyRepo;;;
column372: 23/Jun/2021 7:41 AM;55a972f1726a;oh, somehow double slashes in this web are being replaced with a line break.;;;

column1: Files disappear in "staged files"
column9: SourceTree has a feature that will search the repository root for a file named "icon.png" and if it exist it will use it as an icon in SourceTree UI.This is a great feature that we enjoy and use, however the SourceTree for Windows app keeps a hard reference to that "icon.png" file that prevents the user or git to delete it.a†This becomes a problem if the user want's to checkout another branch in which the "icon.png" doesn't exist, because the file will still be there as a pending change that the user won't be able to remove unless closing SourceTree first (not very intuitive).Trying to discard the change (of the added "icon.png") from SourceTree will just fail and not remove it.Windows File Explorer will alert the user that the file cannot be removed if SourceTree is running.a†Maybe SourceTree could make a copy of the repo's "icon.png" so the reference is to the copy and it doesn't block the original image, or any other strategy to allow image removal.

column1: ssl cerficate
column9: I have configured my remote's push refspec for remote 'user' to push to refs/users/myusername/heads/* such that `git push user` sends my modification to my userspace on my gerrit server.It all works well from the command line, but SourceTree doesn't let me use the default push refspec. It always overrides the target branch with a value, doesn't let me push without a defined target branch and makes it increadibly hard for me to actually find out what it actually does.A solution is obviously a custom button but SourceTree should really honor the push refspec config.
column371: 26/May/2021 2:18 AM;e5e80b87a1d8;I cloned this bug as it's already an issue with v3.4.4 however it doesn't seem like I'm able to edit the Affects Version field.This is still an issue with SourceTree when it comes to Refspec in general (not just gerrit)a†If I set my QA team with their environment to only track QA subdirectory branches in the .git/config i.e[remotea†"origin"]a†a†a†a†urla†=a†git@github.com:org/repo.gita†a†a†a†fetcha†=a†+refs/heads/qa/*:refs/remotes/origin/qa/*a†a†a†a†pusha†=a†refs/heads/*:refs/heads/qa/*a†fetch is respected and Sourcetree would only show the qa/ subdirectory.checkout of a remote branch (doesn't exist locally) Sourcetree would default to using qa/branch-name instead of branch-name (given the remote branch name as origin/qa/branch-name)push works ONLY if the branch already existed in remote, so pushing changes to the branch. However, with a new branch (not published to remote) if I click on Push, the local branch would not be listed in the list of branches that could be pushed.a†Generally with the terminal or most other GUIs I've used, when the refspec is set in .git/config, all interaction would default to those Refspecs, with Sourcetree that doesn't seem to be the case. I'm wondering if there's something I'm missing.;;;

column1: Cannot open a terminal when system git is enabled
column9: (I am not very good at English, so I apologize in advance.)When you try to "git flow finish feature" a Feature branch based on the Support branch, the branch shown in the Preview of the "Finish Feature dialog" is "develop".Example: # You have a tag of v0.0.2 on your master branch. # Run "git flow support v0.0.2 v0.0.2" in a terminal. # Using the gitflow GUI, start the v0.0.2.1 feature based on support/v0.0.2. # Commit any changes to the feature v0.0.2.1. # Try to complete the feature v0.0.2.1 using the gitflow GUI.Expected behavior: The branch shown in the Preview of the "Finish Feature dialog" will be "support/v0.0.2".Actual behavior: The branch that appears in the Preview of the "Finish Feature dialog" will be "develop".Additional information: The actual merge destination will be "support/v0.0.2", which is consistent with the expected behavior. I think it's just that the branch shown in the dialog is wrong.In the config file, the base contains the original branch name when the branch was created using gitflow.It looks like you need to read that and display it.{code:bash}[gitflow "branch.feature/v0.0.2.1"]	base = support/v0.0.2{code}a†

column1: Cannot unstage hunk
column9: Independently of {{.gitignore}}, in the diff tab of the options menu, sourcetree has a setting to exclude certain file patterns from showing a diff. These files still appear in the staged/unstaged files list, but selecting them will not show a preview, the same as if the file were excluded for being eg too large in size. However, this option does not work when the file is untracked.Steps to reproduce: # Create a very large text file and name it {{dump.sql}} # Open the sourcetree options, go to the diff tab and add {{,*dump.sql}} to the ignore file patterns list. # Save the options and restart sourcetree to ensure they take effect. # Reopen sourcetree and select {{dump.sql}} from the unstaged files list. The full file will be loaded. # Stage {{dump.sql}}. # Select {{dump.sql}} from the staged files list. The following message will appear: "No changes in this file have been detected, or it is a binary file or it is configured to be ignored by the File patterns".Expected behaviour:At step 4, the full file is not loaded, and instead the same message from step 6 is displayed.

column1: Sourcetree shuts down foreign pageant process
column9: This bug has existed in every 3.x version of Sourcetree for Windows (currently still present in 3.4.4) and is easily replicated. # Create a repository # Edit the config and add `worktree = C:\\Some\\Path` where that path is not the repository directory. # Create a file in this new main working tree directory # SourceTree sees the new file, but cannot stage it (produces an error) # Stage the file with the terminal and commit with either the terminal or SourceTree # Change the file # SoucreTree sees the change as an edit # Stage the file with SourceTree # It is staged as a file deletion and the actual file in the working tree is DELETED, along with any changes you were attempting to stageDeleting files people are trying to enter into version control is a cardinal sin level of bug and this will be my 3rd time submitting over the past 3 years.
column371: 11/Nov/2021 9:35 PM;f9268144286e;Given the prevalence of this issue in the majority of known git GUIs except GitExtensions, this is probably an issue inside Lib2Git or other common library that is not used by GitExtensions.a†;;;

column1: spell check of commit messages does not work
column9: It seems SourceTree does not work where multiple people try to have instances of SourceTree open at the same time. Relevant piece of info from the log below, it looks like the application tries to create a pipe with the same name, which when multiple people are logged in, appears to clash. One user can start SourceTree, then no-one else can. If the first user closes their instance, a different person may start their instance, however the original person can no longer use their instance.We noticed this on WinServer with RDS, however suspect Win10 with fast user switching would have a similar effect.Using the released 3.4.4 off the website.{{ERROR [2021-04-29 14:22:50,142] [1] [SourceTree.Instances.SingleInstance] [Log] - Error starting first instance pipe server}}{{System.UnauthorizedAccessException: Access to the path is denied.}}{{ at System.IO.__Error.WinIOError(Int32 errorCode, String maybeFullPath)}}{{ at System.IO.Pipes.NamedPipeServerStream.Create(String fullPipeName, PipeDirection direction, Int32 maxNumberOfServerInstances, PipeTransmissionMode transmissionMode, PipeOptions options, Int32 inBufferSize, Int32 outBufferSize, PipeAccessRights rights, SECURITY_ATTRIBUTES secAttrs)}}{{ at System.IO.Pipes.NamedPipeServerStream..ctor(String pipeName, PipeDirection direction, Int32 maxNumberOfServerInstances, PipeTransmissionMode transmissionMode, PipeOptions options, Int32 inBufferSize, Int32 outBufferSize, PipeSecurity pipeSecurity, HandleInheritability inheritability, PipeAccessRights additionalAccessRights)}}{{ at System.IO.Pipes.NamedPipeServerStream..ctor(String pipeName, PipeDirection direction, Int32 maxNumberOfServerInstances, PipeTransmissionMode transmissionMode, PipeOptions options)}}{{ at SourceTree.Instances.SingleInstance.StartServer()}}
column371: 08/Jun/2021 10:49 AM;85824354c561;Hi [~b588430e0d7a],Can you try to turn this setting *RunMultipleInstances*a†to true in youra†*SourceTree.exe.config*.You'll find it here: ..\AppData\Local\SourceTree\app-3.4.4Thanks;;;
column372: 13/Jun/2021 10:39 AM;b588430e0d7a;Hia†I tested with that setting and with multi-users, the issue still occurs with the same error. I confirmed multi-instance was functioning, as each user could now start more than one copy, but more than one instance per user still failed, with same callstack.a†;;;
column373: 14/Jun/2021 6:59 AM;85824354c561;Hey [~b588430e0d7a],There are two clarifications I require here: * Multi user instances, i.e one instance per user is working now or not? * And, multiple instances per user, i.e for the same user, you need more than one instances. May I know what is the reason for this case, and if this one is working or not?Thanks;;;
column374: 14/Jun/2021 1:00 PM;b588430e0d7a;Multiple instances per user is working with that configuration change, however is not the issue we are primarily having trouble with.Multiple user instances - once instance per user for multiple users on the same PC (either Win 10 via Fast User Switching, or Windows Server with RDP sessions) is not functioning, still returning the error as above. Our use case is three different people logging into a terminal server environment due to moving in and out of the office a lot. For these users, only one person may have SourceTree open at a time.;;;
column375: 15/Jun/2021 5:25 AM;85824354c561;I suppose, different users use the machine with their credentials?And each user has their own AppData, with sourcetree installed separately for each user?If that is the case, then Sourcetree should work.If not, then how are you making each user access Sourcetree's location? What's the installation location and what's the AppData path? Is it accessible to all users?;;;
column376: 15/Jun/2021 6:26 AM;b588430e0d7a;Each user has SourceTree installed locally for that user, within their own LocalAppData folders. Individually, each install works just fine, it's only if one user has SourceTree actively open, and then another user attempts to start SourceTree.Based on the callstack in the error log, I'm wondering if perhaps SourceTree is attempting to create the same pipe name for each user? With different credentials in use, if user A creates a pipe named 'SourceTree' in one user account, then it makes sense a second couldn't re-use the same pipe name on the same PC at the same time. Not sure if that would give the specific error in the log though.;;;
column377: 15/Jun/2021 7:32 AM;85824354c561;Hey Scott,I figured it it's not because of the GUID or Name, but due to different access levels which the pipeServer runs.I could replicate this error in the logs by opening sourcetree from one user account, logging off, logging into another account and opening Sourcetree there. And I get the error.But...that doesn't hamper any functionality of sourcetree as far as I could test on both the systems.I wonder why you are facing this trouble.a†a†;;;
column378: 16/Jun/2021 1:31 AM;b588430e0d7a;Not sure what else I can test. It's possible the error is not cause of issues but it's definitely the symptom that if one user's instance cannot create (or access?) the pipe server due to another user account having SourceTree running (thus permission issue), it proceeds to crash.a†;;;
column379: 16/Jun/2021 11:23 AM;85824354c561;It is due to the access levels of the 3 users using sourcetree. I don't think I'll be able to modify any security related configuration from our end. I'll keep this bug open so that anyone in future can use it.Thanks;;;

column1: Problems with Bitbucket Server Integration in Sourcetree Enterprise
column9: a† Hi!Please could you add [WinMerge|https://winmerge.org/] to the merge tool selection as it has supported 3-way merge since [early 2018|https://github.com/WinMerge/winmerge/blob/master/Docs/Users/ChangeLog.md#winmerge-2152---2018-01-28]? Thank you!a†h3. Current situation (Tested on Sourcetree 3.4.3 Windows 64-bit):WinMerge is not available in the "Options > Diff > External Diff / Merge > Merge Tool" dropdown box.h3. Desired outcome:WinMerge is available in thea†"Options > Diff > External Diff / Merge > Merge Tool" dropdown box and merging using it works.a†----h3. Background / related issues:h4. Related bug:The buga†SRCTREEWIN-4572 ("Can't select WinMerge as merge tool") from 2016 was Done because WinMerge didn't yet support a 3-way merge. But as of 2018-01-28 WinMerge has supported 3-way merge (See WinMerge [2.15.2 changelog|https://github.com/WinMerge/winmerge/blob/master/Docs/Users/ChangeLog.md#winmerge-2152---2018-01-28] from 2018-01-28: "Add support for 3-way compare/merge"). I tried today (2021-03-08) using WinMerge 3-way merge with the custom merge option using the information from this [Sourcetree Community question|https://community.atlassian.com/t5/Sourcetree-questions/Can-t-launch-external-merge-tool-WinMerge-on-Windows/qaq-p/315156] and it worked okay.h4. Currently working custom merge options for WinMerge:The options I used in Sourcetree 3.4.3 for Windows 64-bit with WinMerge 2.16.10.0 x64 and which worked for merging: * Options > Diff > External Diff / Merge: ** Merge Tool: Custom ** Merge Command: C:\Program Files\WinMerge\WinMergeU.exe *** Arguments:{noformat}-wl -wr -dl Remote -dm Base -dr Local \"$REMOTE\" \"$BASE\" \"$LOCAL\" -o \"$MERGED\"{noformat}a†

column1: Remote project configuration error when creating a Pull Request after starting app
column9: Being prompted to "Run 'git lfs pull' now?" despite all lfs files up to date when open a tab to a repository.Looks like the implementation ofa†RepoHandlerGitOld.NeedsLargeFilesPull has been not quite right for a while.According to the lfs [source|https://github.com/git-lfs/git-lfs/blob/2e1d981afbe33bd6b9bebb9943daf236636e5e33/commands/command_ls_files.go#L149]a†for the output,a†*ALL* files will be output. Those that exist and are the same size will be annotated with a '*', those that don't with a '-'.{{701ab1713a2cacb797738ab5220a5ed640f1c38affb71e83cc6ac9bd53c35d13 - deletedfile.txt}} {{a6c2fde49d61d6226446ff0fb3d78db15ebae71da9fb505e17a32a1cb4f93ac4 * intactfile.txt}}Currently, SourceTree will prompt if the *first* line matchesa† "[0-9a-fA-F]\{64} . .+", which is always.a†Proposed fix:Change the regex to search for "[0-9a-fA-F]\{64} *-* .+" (changing the first dot to a minus) so that it only looks for files reported by LFS that need to be updated.a†The reason that 3.4.1 doesn't have this issue, is that the *first* file will be investigated for a string that contains "git-lfs.github.com".a†Related to #[13418|http://jira.atlassian.com/browse/SRCTREEWIN-13418]

An attacker could create a malicious repository which, after being cloned in SourceTree for Windows and enabled with git-lfs, is able to exploit this issue to gain code execution on the system.¬†*This is the result of an incomplete fix for CVE-2020-27955**Affected versions:* * Version¬†3.4.2 and earlier¬†*Fix* * You can download the latest version of¬†the [standard installer|https://product-downloads.atlassian.com/software/sourcetree/windows/ga/SourceTreeSetup-3.3.9.exe] or the [enterprise installer|https://product-downloads.atlassian.com/software/sourcetree/windows/ga/SourcetreeEnterpriseSetup_3.3.9.msi].¬†For additional details, see the [full advisory|https://confluence.atlassian.com/display/SOURCETREEKB/SourceTree+for+Windows+Security+Advisory+24th+March+2021]

column1: Sourcetree crashes on launch 99% of the time
column9: The current Sourcetree version 3.4.3 terminates a running pageant process even though it was not started by Sourcetree.The corresponding option to start pageant when Sourcetree is started is disabled. column1: Crash on "Show In Explorer"
column9: When adding a new tag Sourcetree uses the current date on that tag. The tag date is then used by the Github UI for ordering releases. If tags were not added chronologically, the releases are displayed out of sequence.a†This can be avoided by settinga†GIT_COMMITTER_DATE to the date of the commit before adding the tag.a†I suggest adding this as an option under the Advanced Options of the Tag dialog.

column381: 25/Feb/2021 4:44 AM;ae0e47272232;[~3ea269eeadc3], is this prompt happening on choosing Yes?¬† You'll get the popup every time when you open the repo on selecting No, until we implement a checkbox to save the chosen option.;;;
column382: 25/Feb/2021 4:31 PM;bbd6c07fd960;Why was this functionality not needed in previous versions of SourceTree?Before 3.4.2 - SourceTree had no need to pull down LFS each time or ask if we wanted to.¬†;;;
column383: 25/Feb/2021 9:41 PM;3ea269eeadc3;It does seem to still appear even after clicking yes.¬† I also wonder why this prompt needs to exist at all. column1: Select Next Unstaged File when clicking "Stage Selected"
column9: There was an argument injection vulnerability in SourceTree for Windows introduced through git-lfs. An attacker could create a malicious repository which, after being cloned in SourceTree for Windows and enabled with git-lfs, is able to exploit this issue to gain code execution on the system.*Affected versions:* * Versiona†3.3.9a†and earliera†*Fix* * You can download the latest version ofa†the [standard installer|https://product-downloads.atlassian.com/software/sourcetree/windows/ga/SourceTreeSetup-3.3.9.exe] or the [enterprise installer|https://product-downloads.atlassian.com/software/sourcetree/windows/ga/SourcetreeEnterpriseSetup_3.3.9.msi].a†For additional details, see the [full advisory|https://confluence.atlassian.com/display/SOURCETREEKB/SourceTree+for+Windows+Security+Advisory+24th+March+2021]

column9: h3. Issue SummaryA user is reporting that interactive rebase is not possible in 3.4.0.h3. Steps to ReproduceThere are two ways to start an interactive rebase in Sourcetree. The first is to right-click (or context-click) on a commit and choose Rebase children of <sha> interactively‚Ä¶ The second is to pull down the Repository menu and select Interactive rebase.h3. Expected ResultsThe rebase executes.h3. Actual ResultsThe user has reported that there are no interactive rebase GUI capabilities available.h3. WorkaroundCurrently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available

column9: SYMPTOMI have a large project with ~100 tags. I installed on a new system and noticed that while "fetch" from remote worked fine, I could not "push" to remote (bitbucket). It would simply sit there doing nothing then after about a minute I would see "FATAL ERROR: Server unexpectedly Done network connection".a† It doesn't matter what I am pushing - not even a basic commit will work.WORKAROUNDIf I disable "Push all tags to remotes" it works fine.a†a†NOTEThis is NOT specific to the repo. I have another workstation with the same setup, and "Push" works fine, even with "Push all tags... enabled"STEPS: # enable "Push all tags..." # make a change # commit locally # push # observe does not work and eventually gives error message # disable "Push all tags..." # observe works

column1: Unable to open Web Browser Message
column9: *Background:* Since Github is phasing out basic login with password, its important that login with PAT is simple and works for usersa†*Issue:* SourceTree will fail basic login with PAT unless a specific, non-obvious permission is granted (which is not enabled by default in GitHub GUI)a†*Repro:*1. create Personal Access Token from Github developer options with default permissions, or repo permissions2. use PAT as password in SourceTree basic login3. Fails with message to the affect of "user not found"a†Fix: User must check `user:email` permission (not checked by default), otherwise SourceTree will fail login. !image-2020-11-15-15-14-23-474.png!Since its hard to guess this will be needed (and it's disabled by default in Github), it would be great if sourcetree could give a hint on this error i.e. "is \"user:email\" permission set in your PAT?"(Assuming the permission really is necessary for login / common operations.If not, even better would be login succeeding without it)a†NOTE: My SourceTree is version `3.3.6`. The `Affects Version/s` dropdown doesn't accept that, so I selected `3.3.9`

column1: update branch from remote master (as in Github Desktop)
column9: In some situations new files are not shown in the unstaged files with _pending_ active._I'm not sure what these situations are yet._The git command used by sourcetree is:a†`git -c diff.mnemonicprefix=false -c core.quotepath=false --no-optional-locks status --porcelain --ignore-submodules=dirty --untracked-files=all --no-ahead-behind`a†Which includes them prefixed with `A`, however sourcetree refuses to show them in Pending or Untracked, only in *Clean* and All.a†_This is an annoying bug since you may forget to add files._

column1: Repository is not opened if a tab with another repository having the same name already exists
column9: Windows 10.0.18363 x64 - GITSourceTree version 3.3.9I have this very frequent bug lately (multiple times a day). When I view the files diffs (staging or commit), if I scroll down to the bottom of the file, it will freeze the UI and I have to close the process to restart.Not all files trigger this freeze, but when one does, it will freeze everytime when I scrolldown (so I can reproduce the bug easily on my end). It seems related to the Diff "context" since, when I reduce it, the freeze doesn't occur on the same files (behavior change).The freeze is instant, as soon as the Diff panel hits the end-of-file, it freezes.I didn't have this bug in the previous version (3.3.6)

column378: 15/Nov/2020 3:25 PM;e8a2ab645be7;I have the same problem. I also found a workaround.If you get the freeze by scrolling down the diff section to the end (using the mouse wheel), you can click the SourceTree's icon on taskbar to minimize it, then click again to bring the UI back, it will work again.But if you use the down button in the scrollbar to scroll to the end, then SourceTree will freeze for real and there is no way to unfreeze it except using Task Manager to force close it.This freezing issue often occurs when the diff content is fairly short (long just enough to have a scrollbar), I tried by resizing the SourceTree UI to make the scrollbar shorter (viewport becomes smaller than the diff content) and the issue no longer occurs.To avoid this issue completely, you should "drag" the scrollbar by mouse instead of scroll it.Confirmed in version 3.3.9.;;;

column1: Black Dark Theme (rather than Blue)
column9: With the latest Windows build 3.3.9 the integration with my local Bitbucket Server account is not persisted in the repositories and e.g. checking out branches via Bitbucket Server always causes problems because the optional extended integration options for a repository are not saved.Steps to reproduce:1. Change respository extended settings from a the Standard to Bitbucket Server2. Save changes and close Sourcetree3. Open Sourcetree again and verify the extended settings are stored
column371: 15/Oct/2020 2:14 PM;michael.benz;Steps to reproduce: 1. Change respository extended settings from a the Standard to Bitbucket Server !image-2020-10-15-16-13-11-444.png!2. Save changes and close Sourcetree 3. Open Sourcetree again and verify the extended settings are stored--> The warning that no repository details are saved is shown again and the optional extended integration details are back to the "Generic Account" setting !image-2020-10-15-16-14-30-557.png!a†[EDIT] It seems that I do not have the permission to add attachment or embed images. If that is changed I can also provide screenshots for the steps the extended repository menu.[EDIT2] There is a discussion in the community forum going on about "Red Exclamation Mark on Remote Icon"a†https://community.atlassian.com/t5/Sourcetree-questions/Red-Exclamation-Mark-on-Remote-Icon/qaq-p/1174179#U1508296;;;
column372: 26/Oct/2021 11:10 PM;fd3375436755;I followed two suggestions from [https://community.atlassian.com/t5/Sourcetree-questions/Red-Exclamation-Mark-on-Remote-Icon/qaq-p/1174179] . They seem to be effective, but I am waiting to see how persistent the changes are: # User:a†**[baluditor |https://community.atlassian.com/t5/user/viewprofilepage/user-id/3789467]a†- "changed remote account from Generic Account to my GitHub account at the Account in the Repository / Repository Settings" # User:a†[Marit Franks |https://community.atlassian.com/t5/user/viewprofilepage/user-id/3722365] - "Somehow the bookmarks were causing this for me. If you go to View -> Toggle Bookmarks Sidebar and delete the bookmark for the repo in question, then restart sourcetree it should be gone."------I have not attempted the work-around by [rotan_hanrahan |[https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/SRCTREEWIN-10820?focusedCommentId=2664296&page=com.atlassian.jira.plugin.system.issuetabpanels:comment-tabpanel#comment-2664296]|https://jira.atlassian.com/secure/ViewProfile.jspa?name=bc8ce73f79e8]:]{quote}1. Close Sourcetre2. In the project's local .git directory, edit the file *sourcetreeconfig.json* as follows:Replace:{code:java}"RemoteProjectLinks": []{code}With:{code:java}"RemoteProjectLinks": [{"Type":0}]{code}3. Restart SourcetreeOn inspection you will notice that RemoteProjectLinks will be further populated automatically with additional fields that are mainly nulls.This change had no negative impact on the use of Sourcetree to interact with local repository servers, but had the positive impact of removing that annoying misleading red exclamation badge.{quote}a†a†In my journey, I found similar bug reports from at least as far back as 2018. This may not be seen as important enough of an issue to warrant a permanent fix. Hopefully, one or more of the above will help future users. At worst, none of the suggestions appear to be overly destructive.;;;

column405: 21/Jan/2020 5:40 PM;bc8ce73f79e8;It is currently not possible to report this bug against version 3.3.* because the Jira setup does not recognise 3.3.* as a released version. This oversight might explain why nobody is paying any attention to the bug, which has been reported several times against both the Mac and Windows distributions. The 3.3.6 version is not detected when 3.3.4 is told manually to check for updates, suggesting that 3.3.6 has been withdrawn. By whom I do not know. The absence of any comment from anyone in a position of authority is worrying. The fact that all these reports have the "Done" status is similarly worrying.This particular ticket (7108) against the Mac version is actually set to version 3.3b1 (not 3.3.6) by the reporter and the priority is "Low" (despite the symptom severity being "Severity 1 - Critical"). Ticket 12705 against the Windows version mentions 3.3.6 in the description but has the affected version set to 3.2.2, while being Low priority and Severity 2. Similar circumstances appear to affect 12705 and 12766. No doubt many (all?) of the other tickets raised against this issue are similarly misrepresented.This issue is now getting some traction on SO and other places. It's not a good image. It doesn't help that people are erroneously interpreting confirmation of the issue as "solved", which means the reports now appear as "solved" when using search engines. See [https://community.atlassian.com/t5/Sourcetree-questions/Sourcetree-3-3-6-not-updating-UI-content-properly/qaq-p/1270390] as an example, which is marked solved despite (at the time of writing) there being no actual solution. (I do not consider workarounds like pressing F5 all the time to be solutions.)I provide technical guidance to a number of clients, some who are using ST on my recommendation, and I think I will be forced to withdraw such recommendation if this bug persists any longer. That would be a shame as ST has generally been pretty good.Refs:SRCTREEWIN-12578SRCTREEWIN-12705SRCTREEWIN-12766;;;
