column1: Display problems of "unusual" characters in main window diff pane
column9: h4. Environment * Windows 7 Professional 64-bit ** Outdated, I know * Sourcetree v3.3.8.3848h4. Steps to Reproduce # Commit a text file # Write a huge amount of lines (e.g. 20,000 lines) to the file # Add and commit the change # Show "History" pageh4. Current Behavior * Sourcetree tries to display the huge diff fully, and a user can't do anything but just wait for a long time or terminate this app because it never releases the whole control before finishing everythingh4. Appreciated BehaviorAny one (or more) of these: * Sourcetree tries to display the huge diff fully, but a timeout occurs because it took too long time to load ** It would be great if the timeout length can be modified in preferences. * Sourcetree tries to display the huge diff fully, but a user can interrupt it anytime by pressing Esc key or a kind of "Cancel" button * Sourcetree tries to display the huge diff fully, but a user can interrupt it anytime by clicking on another file or commit with light changes because loading is processed only in the diff pane at the lower right * Sourcetree displays diffs partly only within the scrolled area of the diff pane * Sourcetree doesn't start to display diffs automatically before selecting a file in commits ** It's just like the diff pane of the "File Status" page says "Select a file to view the diff" when no files are selected. * Sourcetree doesn't start to display the huge diff automatically because this app knows its hugeness before the loading ** It's just like GitHab says "Large diffs are not rendered by default" when showing commit detail page. * Or another cool solution...

column1: Cannot get past "Registration" step of installer
column9: Suggestion : ability to hide (or disable) the diff pane (or its real time update) in File Status view.Reason : when multi selecting files (in order to stage, discard or whatever action we want to apply), each selection change updates the diff pane content. When those files of big or binary, that update made by the diff can easily freeze SourceTree for multiple seconds, and the bigger you extend your selection, the worse it is.I've been in cases where I had to fight for more than a minute to get a selection of less than 10 files.While it is really unlikely that we want to show multiple files diffs at once in the same view (for ergonomic/readability reason), it is really common to select multiple file to take actions on them. This diff update freeze makes it really painful to apply actions on multiple files in your software.

column9: A silly behaviour I have noticed, for some years now. * Create a new branch in Visual Studio. Create some commits. * Try to push the branch to GitHub in SourceTree by using the 'Push' button. * Check the new branch in the popup. The local name is copied as remote name. * Press 'Push' in the popup. An error is displayed: "The Remote branch '' (Local branch = 'MyName') is invalid. Ref names must follow git ref-format rules: [https://www.kernel.org/pub/software/scm/git/docs/git-check-ref-format.html] Completed with errors, see above." * Now CUT the remote name, and PASTE IT BACK. Now pushing works! I don't know why, I don't see spaces or anything.A silly little trick to get it working. But I must have been banging my head before I ever found it!

column9: When browsing for remote repositories, SourceTree nicely shows the list of all available Github repositories for my account. column373: 25/Nov/2020 10:51 AM;ff3967e4ed5f;I reproduced the same as this issue(version 3.3.9).h3. Issue SummaryWhen authentication failed or no permission to access some stuff in "Remote" tab, Sourcetree crashes after switching to "Remote" tab.h3. Steps to Reproduce*Step 1* Do one of the following steps. - Revoke Authorized OAuth Apps in Sourcetree from GitHub, Bitbucket, etc. - Add Bitbucket(Atlassian) account on Sourcetree, and delete that account*Step 2* - launch Sourcetree application and open "Remote" tab.h3. Expected ResultsShow authentication failed error dialog, don't crash Sourcetree.h3. Actual ResultsCrash Sourcetree applicationThe below exception is thrown in the C:\Users\USERNAME\AppData\Local\Atlassian\SourceTree\sourcetree.log file: - Case: Revoke Authorized OAuth Apps in Sourcetree from GitHub, Bitbucket, etc.{noformat}ERROR [2020-11-25 17:33:29,514] [1] [SourceTree.Model.Repository] [Log] - Repository: [] does not existERROR [2020-11-25 17:33:29,646] [1] [SourceTree.Model.Repository] [Log] - Repository: [] does not existERROR [2020-11-25 17:33:41,288] [1] [SourceTree.Exceptions.RemoteHostException] [Log] - Failed to list organisations for user [*username*]{noformat}h3. WorkaroundDepends on failure reasons. - Case: Revoke Authorized OAuth Apps in Sourcetree from GitHub, Bitbucket, etc. -- -> Give a grant to Sourcetree in Authorized OAuth Apps. - Case: Add Bitbucket(Atlassian) account on Sourcetree, and delete that account from Bitbucket -- -> Delete account setting from "Sourcetree -> Tools -> Options" -- -> If you can't go "Tools -> Options", delete account configuration file( C:\Users\USERNAME\AppData\Roaming\Atlassian\SourceTree\accounts.json ) and reopen Sourcetree.;;;
column374: 17/Jun/2021 10:22 AM;915a3accd9b5;Fixed in 3.4.5 version;;;

column9: Heyho,I set up a shared submodule, and whenever I stage a file in Sourcetree it shows that all files contents are deleted, so effectively Sourcetree states that I am staging a deleted file. When I unstage, the file is deleted. When I add the file via the terminal, it all works flawlessly. I think the tricky thing here is the shared submodule part, with which Sourcetree has trouble. While it could be that I am doing something wrong, I have the belief that Sourcetree is doing more than a simple 'git add' (as that works from the command line).I set up the shared submodule as followed:1. Clone the project that should serve as the shared submodule locally to some directory2. In the shared submodule, modify the local config of this project by adding 'worktree = ..' under [core]For all projects sharing this submodule:3. Add the submodule (this means, effectively I now have two checked out copies of that project)4. Delete the respective submodule folder in ./git/submodule/5. Delete the content of the submodule directory apart from the .git file6. Modify the .git in the submodule directory to point to the path of the project setup in 1)a†(reason for shared submodule is that we have the same code base across different projects, and this way I can change a file in the code base once and it is applied to all projects without having to pull their respective submodule first)
column371: 22/Jul/2020 3:05 PM;40b695fbdae3;See alsoa†[https://community.atlassian.com/t5/Sourcetree-questions/Staging-in-shared-submodule-deletes-file/qaq-p/1046179];;;

column1: Order Center Table
column9: h3. Use CaseIn our project, due to the branching model, most of the merges are none `fast-forward` merges. Therefore, I have set `git config merge.ff false` in my config in case I am merging from the terminal. However, with this setting I am not capable any longer to "force" a `fast-forward` merge from Sourcetree! Even if I uncheck *Create a new commit even if fast-forward is possible*, Sourcetree triggers a "normal" merge thus falling back onto the settings from the git config.h3. Cause of the issueSourcetree calls {{git merge branch-name}}a†whena†*Create a new commit even if fast-forward is possible*a†is not checked:```git -c diff.mnemonicprefix=false -c core.quotepath=false --no-optional-locks merge branch-name```h3. SuggestionBe explicit with the command that is triggered by Sourcetree when unchecking. That is, ...... either make `git merge --ff branch-name` the command which is triggered (instead of `git merge branch-name`),... or even provide a option likea†*Require fast-forward merge*a†triggeringa†`git merge --ff-only branch-name`.

column1: file status is not refreshing
column9: Applies to v3.3.8I had a case where P4Merge was called to resolve a conflict in a too large .js file and it hung in the background. It seemed like nothing had happened so I attempted to resolve again, but got a warning that attempting to use P4Merge on such a large file could cause P4Merge to hang. I opted to not launch the external merge tool. Not having a clear picture what was in my local file, I committed the changes. Also, the file status filter was set to "Modified" so I didn't notice the untracked conflict files. There are multiple areas for improvement here. 1. Detect when external merge tool fails to release conflict files and provide a warning2. Check for presence of unresolved merge conflict files, esp. for recent conflict resolution attempts3. Make "unstaged changes" more visible

column1: unable to use sourcetree to upload local programming into bitbucket
column9: Files encoded with UTF-16 LE (little endian) without BOM (Byte Order Mark) does not display correctly. Every other character is displayed as a space (see screenshot).To reproduce: # Save a text file as "sample.txt" in a git repository with encoding of UTF-16LE (without BOM) ## To do this I use the SublimeText3 "Save with encoding" feature # Add the line{code:java}**/*sample.txt text working-tree-encoding=UTF-16LE{code}to the ".gitattributes" file which should let Git understand how to interpret the file. (this should make it show up correctly) # Look at the file in the diff view in source tree (see screenshot)Screenshot:!image-2020-03-02-09-04-20-243.png!To verify fix: # make sure that the diff view now works with files that are encoded with BOTH of the following encodings: ## UTF-16 LE (without BOM) ## UTF-16 LE BOM (WITH BOM)a†

column376: 19/May/2020 10:21 PM;62ed0ad36a87;I found a fix that works on 3.3.6, and will probably also work on 3.3.8.While poking around I found the log file, and saw an exception failing to load a TimeZoneConverter library that it couldn't find. I went to https://www.nuget.org/packages/TimeZoneConverter/2.3.0 and downloaded the package. It is just a zip file so I extracted timezoneconverter.2.3.0.nupkg\lib\netstandard2.0\TimeZoneConverter.dll and put it into %AppData%\Local\Atlassian\SourceTree\hg_extras, and now it doesn't reset to Generic Account any more! Yay!;;;

column373: 16/Mar/2020 6:22 AM;0467f1c1861a;Seem's I found a simple solution.Open the repository, The Remote-Button is marked with a red exclamation. So far, so bad. But simply open settings of the repository (Buttona† on the right side), settings-Dialog will open. In settings dialog select the remote-path to origin (bitbucket) an click "edit" button below. Than remote details will open. In this dialog simply adjust "Generic Account" to your saved account settings. Click OK and OK again. That's it.Seems newer versions of SCT have problems with GIT Credential Manager.;;;

column1: This is not a valid source path / URL
column9: Configuration for use with gerrit requires modifying a repos origin push to :refs/heads/*:refs/for/*.this worked great with 3.1.3 and earlier versions.a† Now, we just get a push window with absolutely NO branches or anything so it is impossible to push changes unless auto-pushing using the checkmark to push directly upon committing.a† This is ok in normal scenarios, but as soon as you enter complex scenarios like wishing to ammend a checked-out commit that someone else has pending in gerrit, which requires creating a local branch, we normally have to manually tell it to push it to the correct refs/for/correctBranch.a† this is now impossible.a† See screenshots that I will add as comments to the issue to see the 3.1.3 behaviour and the latest behaviour (same in all versions more recent than 3.1.3)Please correct this, it forces our teams to remain on 3.1.3 just to function.

column9: Hello,I recently started using Sourcetree for a fun modding project. I foolishly created a repo of the entire source even though I didn't need most of it.Working on my D partition it had about 20GB space left but the initial commit was around 30GB. Sourcetree did not realize that this was not going to fit for a temporary staging location it tried to create, and eventually filled my SSD's remaining capacity (literally, 0 bytes remaining) The staging process then failed due to this but somehow still exited with a green status code.Since then I'm able to use my local repo even if I made it absurdly large for my purposes, but every screen hangs and no files I stage or commit or any commits I make update the context at all, though that may have already been an issue and I just didn't notice it before.¬†However, now my D partition had 97 GB of "Temporary files" that I can't locate and as far as I know aren't Windows files (C is my OS partition). Please help me find and delete any remnants of this failed temp copy action made by Git.MY version of Sourcetree says it's Version 3.3.6, why can't I choose it in the dropdown? O,o!image-2020-01-27-22-42-59-024.png!¬†

column1: Allow branch names that start with '/'
column9: Same as before: https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/SRCTREEWIN-7168It says fixed but you destroyed it again in version 3, apparently.h3. Problem DefinitionSourceTree gets installed ina†_{{%localappdata%\SourceTree\}}_a†folder and it's not possible for the users to change the installation location.Some users would like to be able to do so, e.g. because this folder is protected by an antivirus and to start the program, they have to disable the scanner once.h3. Suggested SolutionAllow users to select the installation location for SourceTree.h3. AdditionallyWhat's up with programs lately just installing themselves into appdata and in C:\Program FIles\ without asking? Why do you think you can decide for me where I want to install it? Chrome, Discord, Sourcetree and some othera†impudent programs that think they know better. You don't know better. I have 3 places where I install my programs:a Ę Windows drive C:\ on SSD for drivers and the most core stuff like .NET and C++ redistributables, Windows updates.a Ę Second SSD drive D:\ for programs that require performance: UE4 editor, UE4 Engines, Visual Studio, etc.a Ę And then I have hard drive G:\ for all other programs.Sourcetree would go on the HDD because it doesn't require good performance SSDs provide and it isn't a core part of the system.Let us install programs where we want, not where you want.

column1: Remote not showing private GitHub repos
column9: Configuration for use with gerrit requires modifying a repos origin push to :refs/heads/*:refs/for/*.this worked great with 3.1.3 and earlier versions.¬† Now, we just get a push window with absolutely NO branches or anything so it is impossible to push changes unless auto-pushing using the checkmark to push directly upon committing.¬† This is ok in normal scenarios, but as soon as you enter complex scenarios like wishing to ammend a checked-out commit that someone else has pending in gerrit, which requires creating a local branch, we normally have to manually tell it to push it to the correct refs/for/correctBranch.¬† this is now impossible.¬† See screenshots that I will add as comments to the issue to see the 3.1.3 behaviour and the latest behaviour (same in all versions more recent than 3.1.3)Please correct this, it forces our teams to remain on 3.1.3 just to function.

column9: The option "Refresh automatically when files change" no longer works in 3.3.7. It was working fine in 3.3.6. What used to happen was when SourceTree gained focus, if anything had changed it would auto refresh. This no longer occurs. (NOTE: option "Refresh when application is not in focus" is disabled)In fact even operations initiated in Sourcetree no longer refresh. For example creating or applying a stash doesn't actually update the repo view, you have to manually hit F5 to force a refresh. Again this was working fine in 3.3.6.This is causing major issues for us as it means you are operating in a stale view of the repo unless you keep constantly hitting F5 to force a full refresh.

column1: Sourcetree reproducable fails for a basic use case (open working directory)
column9: SourceTree exhibits a very high CPU usage when typing into the commit message window. It can be easily demonstrated simply by running SourceTree, opening an empty repository (just git init and nothing else), opening the File Status and typing random text very quickly. It gets worse with time, i.e. if I leave SourceTree running overnight, in the morning I don't even need to type too quickly, just a regular typing will be able slow down SourceTree significantly - I might see speeds as slow as two characters per second entered into the Message window, which can be quite a pain if I need to provide a detailed description of a commit. The 2.x versions were fine in this aspect, and I think even 3.1 was reasonable; 3.2 and 3.3 are not reasonable.

column9: When starting Sourcetree, the remote button is shown with an exclamation annotation, supposedly because Sourcetree does not know how to open the remote in a browser. column380: 16/Jan/2020 4:24 PM;605c32d09711;Based on feedback from other users you can try a few options, but they are temporary in my experience. The issue can reoccur at any point. Here are the options: # If the issue only affects the left-most tab, move a different less-used tab to the left-most position and restart SourceTree. # Close and re-open all tabs, the issue should disappear (at least for a time) # Remove and re-add all bookmarks in SourceTree (just the bookmarks, don't delete the repositories).The third option is currently working for me and hasn't re-occurred, but I'm not totally convinced yet.;;;

column402: 22/Jan/2020 7:07 AM;4ac7de995b3a;I see where you are coming from Kira, but when a tool breaks to the point of silently showing (and acting on) an incorrect state it _is_ critically broken.Let's all keep our fingers crossed that Atlassian will fix this soon. For example take a look at this old classic from JIRA:{quote}We agree that this is something that should be changed and because of this, I am reopening this suggestion. [...]a†this would be the right thing to do.{quote}a†That official comment is almost 4 years old, on an [issue|https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRACLOUD-25060] that is almost 9 years old about a logical feature request to allow the users to rename file attachments in JIRA.;;;
column403: 22/Jan/2020 7:52 AM;5f2a450116da;Well, I won't argue on anything that increases the likelihood of this getting fixed, and if it was possible to vote for this to get elevated to critical priority, you would have my vote. =^,^=a†Point being though, the point of having priorities in the first place is that not everything can be highest priority, and the priority of a potential "product does no longer work"-bug should obviously be higher than the priority of a "product still works, but the usability is greatly impeded"-bug. Thus, if this bug is critical priority, then there has to be a super-critical priority for bugs that are even worse, and I can easily imagine such a bug, for example "pushing to repository no longer works". Now THAT would be a true show stopper.a†That having been said, I wholeheartedly agree that having this bug on low priority is unacceptable, and that the fact that it has not been fixed for over a *month* now is poor form. But things don't seem to be running well with Atlassian right now anyways. For example, the new Confluence Editor is ineffably horrible and there is no option to revert to the old one. Or in short, I wouldn't be surprised if this is the beginning of the end. In any case, I'll be encouraging my company to transition to non-Atlassian products.;;;

column1: UX improvement
column9: Set filter for "Show Remote Branches" off, or branch filter to "Current Branch".Then, try to "Jump to" a specific revision that is not visible due to the filter. SourceTree starts loading commits and will never end the load.It should yield an error message telling us that the commit is not visible due to filters instead.

column380: 16/Jan/2020 4:20 PM;605c32d09711;Based on feedback from other users you can try a few options, but they are temporary in my experience. The issue can reoccur at any point. Here are the options: # If the issue only affects the left-most tab, move a different less-used tab to the left-most position and restart SourceTree. # Close and re-open all tabs, the issue should disappear (at least for a time) # Remove and re-add all bookmarks in SourceTree (just the bookmarks, don't delete the repositories).The third option is currently working for me and hasn't re-occurred, but I'm not totally convinced yet.;;;

column1: Repair stash External Diff when diff tool is custom
column9: SourceTree may not allow the user to open a local repository, and will instead select another tab that is already open. This happens when the repo is also a submodule of another repo and the submodule is already open in another tab. Steps to reproduce:Git repository 'A' cloned to C:\GitRoot\AGit repository 'B' cloned to C:\Git\Root\BRepo B is also a submodule of A, so a copy also exists in C:\GitRoot\A\B.Open repo A.In Submodules section of repo A, double-click repo B to open it in another tab.Click '+' to the right of tabs. In Local Repositories double-click repository B (i.e. the clone in C:\GitRoot\B).A new tab should open for repo B, however the repo B (submodule) tab is selected instead.This is a fairly new bug in SourceTree that affects version {color:#ffbdad}+*3.3.4*+{color:#172b4d} (which is not available in the Jira Affected Version/s field).{color}{color}

column1: When looking closer at the available seats the screen may stop scrolling in the expected way
column9: I originally could not create a branch due to errors in the sourcetree. At first I thought i made a mistake, After checking out .gitignore and readme.md, I found that sourcetree was not consistent when enabling me to create a branch. After throughly testing by reinstalling sourcetree and removing readme.md and .git folder, it has been confirmed that sourcetree is not totally stable or consistent. There's no guarantee that I'm able to create a branch or it can suddenly work even when not all commands in git was used. Please reply so I can show you the error message that can no longer be reproduced.¬†
